danger_signals.meaning: Loss of offensive capability; vulnerability to opponent pressure

danger_signals.visual_form: Character stagger animation with visual indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Stunned condition allowing free opponent attacks; critical vulnerability window

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific ground areas with environmental features
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental kill zones; defeat in certain locations triggers stage-specific finishing moves

danger_signals.visual_form: Character holding back/down position with visual stance change
danger_signals.meaning: Opponent preparing special move or meter recovery; incoming threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Character retreating animation with defensive stance
safety_signals.meaning: Active meter recharge mode; temporary reduced offensive threat but maintained positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Character facing opponent with guard raised
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation; pressing away (high blocks) or down-away (low blocks)

safety_signals.visual_form: Character positioned away from opponent on stage
safety_signals.meaning: Safety from melee attacks; outside immediate threat range
Offensive capability available; readiness for special moves and taunt pressure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character costume and equipment reflecting historical period
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Same character sprites maintain consistent visual identity regardless of opponent matchup
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage backgrounds reflect character's era (e.g., futuristic cityscapes for 2030 A.D. fighters, historical settings for 1899 A.D. characters)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Visible weapons or martial arts stance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fighters carrying weapons display equipment in idle and attack animations; unarmed fighters use hand-to-hand combat poses
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character model variations indicate combat philosophy and historical role

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character design emphasizes heroic qualities rather than villainous traits
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No visual distinction between 'good' and 'evil' factions—all characters are potential heroes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Character backstory (shown in endings) reflects positive historical impact rather than destructive outcomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Characters assembled in fighting arena stages; opponent selection screen shows bracket progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All fighters motivated by single resurrection opportunity; tournament rules enforce one-on-one combat; competitive stance and aggressive positioning

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Character at Risk of Historical Death
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character epilogue text and ending sequence revealed upon victory; visual narrative of character's fate prevented
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character fighting with heightened desperation; taunt animations suggest determination to survive

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage backgrounds render period-appropriate architecture, technology, and setting details
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Overkill finishing moves utilize stage-specific hazards reflecting historical era's killing methods (environmental storytelling through lethality)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Power Balance Shift (Special Attack Meter Dynamics)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Meter depletion visible on HUD; character stance changes when meter exhausted vs. when full
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive retreats when meter depleted; aggressive offensives when meter full; taunt execution drains opponent's meter, creating psychological pressure shifts

environmental_storytelling.example: Background environmental elements kill defeated opponents based on stage location—reflects historical era's danger and setting lethality without impacting narrative (canonical deaths still occur per history)

Jonathan Blade wears 2030 A.D. futuristic tactical gear; Jetta Maxx displays 1899 A.D. Russian aristocratic styling—visual design communicates temporal displacement and character identity without dialogue
Stage environments render historically-appropriate architecture, technology levels, and aesthetic details; visual context reinforces character's temporal origin and historical significance
Defeating arcade mode reveals epilogue showing character avoiding death and making positive historical impact; visual narrative resolution confirms character's redemption and world-state improvement

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon Visibility as Character Archetype Signal
environmental_storytelling.example: Some fighters carry visible weapons (swords, guns, tools) while others rely on martial arts; equipment presence communicates combat specialty and historical role without tutorial exposition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responsive to input; directional movement (away/toward/jump/crouch); attack chains vary by button press (weak/medium/strong with speed-damage tradeoff)
Stagger animation; momentary vulnerability; opponent can land guaranteed free attack; visual disorientation signal
Cannot execute special moves; reverts to basic attacks; can enter defensive shell to recharge; forced passive positioning
Retreating stance; meter recharging; reduced offensive capability; visual indicator of tactical reset
Can execute special moves beyond meter zero (difficulty scaling); uses taunt to drain player meter; adapts pressure based on player spacing
May increase special move frequency; visual aggression escalation as defeat approaches
Character defeat in specific ground location triggers background element animation; environmental hazard executes defeated opponent; visual finisher plays out regardless of narrative continuity

notes: Eternal Champions communicates world-state primarily through: (1) Character-era visual design establishing temporal faction; (2) Special Attack Meter as real-time resource pressure signal; (3) Stage-specific Overkills as environmental danger manifestation without narrative impact; (4) Dizzy state as visual vulnerability condition; (5) Victory epilogues as world-state resolution narrative. The game uniquely positions all fighters as morally heroic, eliminating traditional villain visual markers—faction identity is temporal rather than moral. Background authenticity and character costume design carry heavy environmental storytelling weight. The mechanic of computer opponents ignoring meter limits in tournament mode signals difficulty scaling rather than rule-breaking, communicated behaviorally rather than visually.
